jobs.description: The Senior Audit Manager is responsible for managing audit assurance activities across key Citibank UAE businesses and products, including Wealth, Services, Markets and Banking, while also overseeing Citibank UAE cross-discipline audits. Contribute to continuous improvement of audit assurance processes, including development of data analytics and usage of AI tools to enhance audit efficiency and coverage. jobs.description: The Business Execution Analyst will be a pivotal member of the franchise's first line of defense, responsible for driving operational excellence, strengthening the control environment, and ensuring seamless coordination of key governance and regulatory processes. This role requires a proactive and detail-oriented individual who can effectively manage multiple priorities, liaise with various stakeholders, and provide critical support to the Business Execution team and senior leadership. The successful candidate will play a key role in upholding the integrity of the franchise's risk and control framework. Key Responsibilities: I. Governance & Committee Coordination Meeting Management: Drive the end-to-end preparation and coordination for Country Coordinating Committee (CCC) meetings. This includes compiling, reviewing, and distributing executive-level materials, preparing presentation slides, and collating essential data for discussion. Action Item Tracking: Diligently track, monitor, and follow up on all tasks and action items arising from CCC meetings to ensure timely completion and resolution by the responsible parties. Annual Survey Coordination: Lead the coordination and execution of the annual CCC survey, managing the process from distribution to data analysis and the presentation of findings. Documentation: Take ownership of maintaining and updating critical documents, records, and charters owned by the franchise. II. Issue & Risk Management Lifecycle Oversight: Manage downloading issue data from the management dashboard, tracking progress, and following up with relevant teams to ensure timely activation and closure. Proactive Escalation: Provide robust oversight for all issues and Corrective Action Plans (CAPs) under the CCOBH ownership. Proactively identify and escalate potential risks, such as upcoming target dates, potential re-targets, or re-opened issues. Remediation Support: Work closely with business units to ensure all necessary approvals are secured for any issue re-targets. Collaborate with second line-of-defense functions and other partners to escalate at-risk items and secure support for remediation efforts. III. Control Environment & MCA Program Control Enhancement: Actively contribute to strengthening the overall control environment by partnering with business leaders to increase the rate of self-identified issues and foster a culture of proactive risk management. MCA Program Support: Download and analyze Country MCA reports to support the franchise in the effective monitoring and testing of key controls. Overdue Controls Management: Focus on reducing the number of overdue Regulatory Changes(RCs) to achieve a tangible reduction in compliance risk. This includes monitoring overdue RCs across the country and driving the relevant Assessment Units (AUs) toward timely resolution. ICAPs Integration: In line with Citi’s Issue Management standards, ensure that overdue RCs are appropriately escalated by following up with relevant AUs to open an official issue/CAP in the ICAPs system in a timely manner. IV. Regulatory Compliance & Engagement Deliverable Tracking: Proactively track, coordinate, and manage all regulatory deliverables to ensure deadlines are met and submissions are of high quality. Communication Logging: Meticulously maintain the regulatory communication tracker, ensuring all interactions with regulatory bodies are logged accurately and promptly. Advisory Support: Support the Independent Compliance Risk Management (ICRM) team, as applicable, in the determination and application of regulatory requirements to a specific business or function. Stakeholder Engagement: Assist the CCOBH in preparing for and managing engagements with regulatory bodies, governmental agencies, and other external stakeholders as required. jobs.description: Job Summary AECOM is seeking an experienced Materials Inspector for a major Roads and Infrastructure, NDRC, Stormwater, sewerange lines projects located in Dubai. The role involves both inspection and the supervision of Roads and Infrastructure works of the project and to ensure that the activities are being executed in accordance with the project specification. • Inspect site conditions prior to the start of construction activities to verify the compatibility of the site with the approved design and permanent works. • Check for obstructions and document existing site conditions through photographs to prevent potential damage to existing utilities, structures, and landscaping. • Review and verify materials delivered to the site for conformance with approved samples, specifications, and equipment lists; report any non-conformances to the Resident Engineer (RE). • Ensure that all materials are properly stored and protected at the site to maintain quality and prevent damage or contamination. • Inspect construction of both temporary and permanent works to confirm compliance with contract documents, approved shop drawings, and project specifications. • Prepare and maintain accurate daily inspection logs, test reports, and site records in accordance with the Contract Administration Procedures Manual. • Monitor site safety practices and ensure that all works are carried out following approved safety procedures; report unsafe practices or incidents to the RE immediately. • Issue Non-Conformance Reports (NCRs) and Site Observation Reports (SORs) for any deviation from approved materials, specifications, or procedures, and follow up on corrective actions. • Assist in the inspection of completed works, prepare punch lists, and monitor testing, commissioning, and handover activities to ensure quality compliance. • Review and provide recommendations on Contractors’ Requests for Information (RFIs) related to civil materials and testing requirements. • Evaluate requests for material variances or substitutions and provide technical comments and recommendations to the RE for approval. • Read and interpret working drawings and related specifications, enforce compliance on-site, and prepare inspection and material testing reports. • Coordinate with third-party testing laboratories and ensure all material tests are performed as per project specifications and standards. • Ensure that all material approvals, test results, NCRs, and inspection records are properly documented and maintained for monthly reporting and project close-out. • Coordinate with relevant authorities for material approvals, testing, and compliance verification.   Bachelor’s Degree or Diploma in Relevant Technical Field 5 + years of relevant experience in laboratory materials testing including field inspection of samples and materials. Middle East experience preferred DCL  approval is preferred Relevant experience in comparable roads, NDRC, Stormwater, sewerage lines, pump station, irrigation works etc.

We’re now entering our next chapter — one powered by AI. We’re looking for AI talent: curious problem-solvers who know how to apply AI to build tools, automate workflows, and create real impact. jobs.description: Job Responsibilities Manage and oversee all construction activities related to the programme, ensuring compliance with approved designs, specifications, quality standards, and contractual requirements. Lead and supervise the site supervision team and monitor contractor performance, progress, and resource deployment. Coordinate with contractors, stakeholders, utility providers, and authorities to ensure efficient project delivery. Identify and mitigate construction risks, resolve site issues, and support timely decision making. Ensure compliance with health, safety, environmental, and quality requirements. Monitor project progress and support the successful testing, commissioning, and handover of works. Minimum Requirements Minimum 15 years of experience in the management of design and/or construction of major infrastructure projects. Proven experience as a Construction Manager on at least one project of similar size and complexity. Experience managing multidisciplinary construction and site supervision teams. Strong knowledge of construction management, contract administration, quality assurance, and health and safety requirements. Middle East experience is highly advantageous.   Bachelor's degree in Civil Engineering or a related discipline from an accredited university. Master's degree is preferred. Chartered Engineer status or equivalent professional accreditation is highly desirable.

jobs.title: Inspector - Material
jobs.description: Inspect site conditions prior to start of construction activities. Verify the compatibility of the site with the designed permanent works. Check for obstructions. Photograph and document the condition of existing property that might be damaged by the work. Make sure all existing structures, property, utilities and landscaping are properly protected. Review materials delivered to site for conformance with approved samples, materials and equipment and report findings to RE. Check that materials delivered to site are properly stored and report discrepancies to the RE. Inspect the construction of temporary and permanent works to ensure compliance with the Contract Documents and approved shop drawings and submittals Prepare daily logs and reports. Maintain records of inspections made, tests performed, work executed, etc perform all work in accordance with the Contract Administration Procedures Manual. Observe site safety practices and the progress of the work. Report any concerns to the RE. Assists with the inspection of completed works. Help prepare punch lists. Monitor the start-up, testing, commissioning and handover of the works. Review RFIs received from Contractor (s) and make recommendations on resolution. Review any requests for variance received from Contractors and provide comments as to entitlement and recommendations on resolution. Able to read working drawings and related specifications; be able to enforce these specifications and produce relevant reports. Bachelor’s Degree or Diploma in Relevant Technical Field. RTA or DM approval is must

jobs.title: Engineer - Planning
jobs.description: Develop, review, and maintain detailed project schedules (baseline, recovery, and revised programs) using Primavera P6 or equivalent planning software. Prepare master schedules, look-ahead programs, and weekly/monthly progress reports in coordination with project teams. Monitor project progress against the approved baseline and identify delays, risks, and critical path impacts. Perform schedule risk analysis, delay analysis, and time impact analysis (TIA) in accordance with contract requirements. Coordinate with construction, procurement, design, and commercial teams to ensure alignment between schedule, resources, and deliverables. Review contractors’ planning submissions, method statements, and manpower histograms for compliance with contract specifications. Support the Project Manager/Resident Engineer by providing planning inputs for decision-making and corrective actions. Prepare and evaluate EOT (Extension of Time) claims, substantiating delays with proper planning narratives and evidence. Track procurement, long-lead items, approvals, and permits to ensure schedule compliance. Develop recovery and mitigation plans to bring delayed activities back on track. Conduct weekly and monthly planning meetings with stakeholders and issue meeting minutes related to schedule actions. Ensure integration of cost, cash flow, and resource-loaded schedules where required. Maintain proper documentation and ensure planning records comply with project controls and QA/QC procedures. Provide mentoring and guidance to junior planning engineers and site planners. Liaise with clients, consultants, and authorities on all schedule-related matters. Support project close-out by preparing as-built programs and final planning reports.

jobs.title: Material Inspector (Roads and Infrastructure)
jobs.description: • Inspect and verify all construction materials delivered to site to ensure compliance with RTA specifications, approved material submittals, and contract requirements . • Check material source approvals, delivery notes, batch certificates, and test reports prior to acceptance and use at site. • Carry out sampling and witnessing of laboratory and field tests for soil, aggregates, concrete, asphalt, steel, and other civil materials as per approved ITPs. • Ensure materials used for roadworks, bridges, culverts, drainage, and utility works conform to approved specifications and drawings. • Monitor storage, handling, and protection of materials to prevent contamination, segregation, or damage. • Verify compliance with RTA-approved suppliers, manufacturers, and testing laboratories . • Coordinate with the Material Engineer and Site Engineer regarding testing schedules, approvals, and material-related issues. • Inspect asphalt paving, concrete works, earthworks, and backfilling to ensure correct materials and workmanship are used. • Review and submit Work Inspection Requests (WIRs) related to materials and civil works. • Maintain accurate inspection records, test results, and material logs for QA/QC and as-built documentation. • Identify non-conforming materials, raise NCRs and site observations , and follow up on corrective actions. • Ensure compliance with HSE requirements , especially during material handling, sampling, and testing activities. • Monitor material usage and quantities to avoid wastage, misuse, or deviation from approved specifications . • Assist in RTA inspections and audits , providing required documentation and site support. • Report daily site activities, material inspections, and testing status to the Material Engineer / Resident Engineer . Bachelor’s Degree or Diploma in Relevant Technical Field RTA/ DCL  approval is preferred 7+ years of relevant experience in laboratory materials testing including field inspection of samples and materials. Middle East experience preferred Relevant experience in comparable roads and bridges projects
